highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.105 Released: Worktree Navigation, PreCompact Hooks & More
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.105 adds a `path` parameter to the `EnterWorktree` tool so you can switch directly into any existing Git worktree. It also introduces PreCompact hook support, letting hooks block context compaction by exiting with code 2 or returning a block decision — giving you finer control over long sessions. Background monitor support for plugins was also included in this release.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.101: Team Onboarding Command & Enterprise TLS Support
highlights.summary: The new `/team-onboarding` command automatically generates a ramp-up guide for teammates based on your local Claude Code usage — great for sharing workflows across a team. Enterprise users benefit from OS CA certificate store trust by default, meaning TLS proxies now work without manual configuration. The `/ultraplan` command also received updates in this release.

highlights.title: Claude Code OAuth Outage: Login Broken for 12+ Hours on v2.1.107
highlights.summary: Many users on Claude Code v2.1.107 are reporting that the OAuth login flow is broken — the terminal gets stuck at `Paste code here if prompted >` with no way to paste the code. This appears to affect both Windows and Linux users and has been under-reported outside of Reddit and Discord communities. If you are unable to log in, check the linked GitHub issue and HN thread for workarounds and status updates.

highlights.title: Vercel Plugin for Claude Code Reads Your Prompts — Privacy Concern Raised
highlights.summary: A developer discovered that the official Vercel plugin for Claude Code collects telemetry that includes user prompts, raising privacy concerns for anyone using it in professional or sensitive projects. Before installing third-party Claude Code plugins, it is worth reviewing their permissions and telemetry settings. This is a good reminder to audit MCP plugins and extensions for data collection practices.

highlights.title: Depwire: Codebase Dependency Graph & MCP Server for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Depwire is an open-source tool that builds a visual dependency graph of your codebase and exposes it as an MCP server, so AI assistants like Claude Code can better understand project structure. This can significantly improve the accuracy of Claude Code's responses in large or complex repos by giving it structured context about how modules relate. It is a straightforward integration for teams looking to get more precise AI assistance.

highlights.title: The Ultimate Claude Code Guide: MCP, Skills & More
highlights.summary: This YouTube video offers a comprehensive walkthrough of Claude Code's most powerful features, including MCP integrations and skill customization. It is a solid watch for developers who have gotten past the basics and want to unlock more advanced workflows. Watching this alongside the official docs is a great way to see real-world usage in action.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: A concise 30-minute video tutorial designed to take you from zero to productive with Claude Code, covering the essential commands and patterns used daily. It is especially useful for developers who prefer video learning over reading documentation. The tight runtime means you can fit it into a lunch break and come away with actionable knowledge.

tips_of_the_day: Before installing any Claude Code plugin or MCP extension, check its README or source code for telemetry or data-collection settings — your prompts may contain sensitive code you don't want shared.

Use the new `/team-onboarding` command after a few weeks of using Claude Code on a project to auto-generate a guide for new teammates — it captures your real workflow patterns rather than generic docs.

If you're working across multiple Git worktrees, pair the updated `EnterWorktree` tool with a PreCompact hook to prevent Claude Code from compacting context mid-task when switching branches, keeping your session history intact.
